Notes.title: Home Turf
Notes.description: Campus has a nice ~2.5 mile loop around the perimeter. The Norwottuck Rail Trail is superb to be with the nature. The hills around the area are also fun to run for training.

Notes.title: Daffodil Run 10K
Notes.description: Ran with Angela around Amherst neighborhood.

Notes.title: Boston Marathon Spectating
Notes.description: On Patriots day I would go to the baseball game then spectate the Boston Marathon. The energy and excitement of the crowd is amazing, and it's inspiring to see runners from all over the world pushing themselves to the limit.

Notes.title: Boston 5K
Notes.description: The crowd was amazing. There's always something special about the marathon finish line.

Notes.title: Boston 10K
Notes.description: Had a pretty bad injury leading up to this race. Did not really train much leading up to the race. My PT gave me a green light to run but I was still pretty nervous. It was super humid that day, but somehow I ended up running a pretty solid time considering the circumstances, and it felt great to be back out there racing again.

Notes.title: Boston Half Marathon
Notes.description: Felt strong leading up to the race, and did well! The course was super pretty and the weather was perfect. My labmates came out to cheer me on which was really nice. I ended up running a personal best time for the half marathon, and it was a great confidence boost for my training.

Notes.title: Visiting a Friend
Notes.description: Did couple runs when I was here. Mostly indoors because of the air quality and traffic.

Notes.title: Home
Notes.description: I didn't know much about running when I was here. I would go for a run here and there but I didn't really have a consistent routine. The weather was humid but the heat is barable.

Notes.title: Central Park
Notes.description: The classic NYC running experience

Notes.title: Golden Gate Park
Notes.description: Had a good time running there. This park is very nice for running!

Notes.title: JP Morgan Corporate Challenge
Notes.description: Race with my internship team!

Notes.title: Wester Mass 10
Notes.description: My first long distance race. Did not train much for this race, but this is where I started to get more routinely into running. The course was from Amherst to Northampton.

Notes.title: Pelham Strip
Notes.description: Ran here during the summer of 2025. Super nice neighborhood to run in. Super hilly as well.

Notes.title: Hartford Marathon
Notes.description: My first marathon! So glad my friends came out to support me. The course was pretty hilly and the weather was not great, but I ended up finishing and it was a great experience overall. Shout out to Stephanie for flying out to support me.

Notes.title: My after work training spot
Notes.description: San tomas aquino trail everyday after work. See the same people running there every day. It was nice while it lasted

Notes.title: The bean loop
Notes.description: Ran around Stephanie's neighborhood. When I spent my Christmas break there. The loop looks like a literal bean.

Notes.title: My first half marathon
Notes.description: I was going to miss my long run that week anyway, but I saw that there was a half marathon happening in DC and I thought it would be fun to do it.

Notes.title: Eurosys'24 brought me to Athens!
Notes.description: Appreciation run around the city after the conference. I got to run in the Panathenaic Stadium where the first modern Olympics was held. It was a pretty cool experience to run there and see the history of the place. Also got to see the torch lightning ceremony for the Olympics which was pretty cool as well.

Notes.title: My second half marathon
Notes.description: Ran this race two weeks after wisdom teeth removal. I did not have a good time, but managed to finish.

Notes.title: e-Energy'24 brought me to Singapore!
Notes.description: How I miss this place. I got to run around the Marina Bay area and see the iconic skyline of Singapore.

Notes.title: Singapore 10K
Notes.description: Super humid 10K. I was not really prepared for the humidity and it was a struggle. Had a super nice meal with my friends after the race!

Notes.title: Chicago 5k
Notes.description: Ran with my Aunties!

Notes.title: New Year Resolution 10K
Notes.description: My mom wants to see me run a 10K before the end of the year, so I signed up for her to use the cowbells.

Notes.title: Summit Run 5k
Notes.description: The slogan of this race is 'just one hill' but that one hill is BRUTAL.

Notes.title: Calabasas means pumpkin
Notes.description: The loop in park looks like a pumpkin.

Notes.title: Fremont Older Trail
Notes.description: Angela showed me this trail and it's a nice hilly trail to run on. Also, leaves of three, stays away from me.

Notes.title: Workout with brother
Notes.description: Ran with my brother when visited him in Raleigh.

Notes.title: Los Angeles Marathon
Notes.description: Sunny California at its literal meaning. It has been a windy week leading up to the race, but ended up being the hottest day of the week on the race day. Had a great time despite the heat and the elevation. Amazing crowd. Shout out to Stephanie for being there for me, not just at the race but the entire week. This one is for you :)

Notes.title: Los Angeles 5K
Notes.description: 5K with Stephanie! We have been wanting to run together for a while and we finally made it happen. The course was around the Dodger Stadium. Cold at the start but warmed up pretty quickly. It was a little dusty, but we had a great time :)

Notes.title: Redondo Beach
Notes.description: Sunrise shakeout run along the beach. The view was gorgeous.
